source_name: Code4rena Noya Findings and Analysis Report (2024-04)
finding_title: Value of asset token can be incorrect when usage of ETH/USD Chainlink oracle is needed
summary: There are tokens that have token/ETH but no token/USD Chainlink oracles currently in which these tokens have the in scope token behaviors described in https://code4rena.com/audits/2024-04-noya and should be supported by this protocol. To support these tokens, the ETH/USD Chainlink oracle can be used as a part of the route set by the following NoyaValueOracle.updatePriceRoute function for converting the token amount to ETH first and then converting the converted ETH amount to USD.
root_cause: incorrect accounting or validation logic in Value of asset token can be incorrect when usage of ETH/USD Chainlink oracle is needed, causing the protocol to compute or enforce the wrong state transition
broken_invariants: oracle valuation must remain correct under the root cause: incorrect accounting or validation logic in Value of asset token can be incorrect when usage of ETH/USD Chainlink oracle is needed, causing the protocol to compute or enforce the wrong state transition
oracle, TVL, and collateral valuation must use correctly scaled, fresh, and manipulation-resistant inputs
state changes must remain internally consistent across all affected accounting buckets and external integrations
attack_preconditions: the affected component is deployed or configured in the way described by the final report
the report-described user, keeper, vault, connector, oracle, or liquidation flow is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, keeper, liquidator, vault participant, or protocol-integrated actor able to trigger the affected flow
trigger_actions: execute the report-described function sequence or state transition
observe mispricing, accounting drift, value loss, fund lock, or denial of service

impact.description: There are tokens that have token/ETH but no token/USD Chainlink oracles currently in which these tokens have the in scope token behaviors described in https://code4rena.com/audits/2024-04-noya and should be supported by this protocol. To support these tokens, the ETH/USD Chainlink oracle can be used as a part of the route set by the following NoyaValueOracle.updatePriceRoute function for converting the token amount to ETH first and then converting the converted ETH amount to USD.

why_not_higher: already classified as High in the final C4 report
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with public report evidence and affected code/finding references
common_false_positive_angles: verify the same component and configuration are in scope
check whether the issue is reachable by an untrusted or economically motivated actor
confirm the impact is more than a view-only mismatch, dust loss, or privileged-only misconfiguration
mitigations: getValueFromChainlinkFeed(AggregatorV3Interface(primarySource), amount, getTokenDecimals(decimalsSource), isPrimaryInverse) returned by the ChainlinkOracleConnector.getValue function can be further divided by 10 ** 10 when asset is ETH, baseToken is USD, and isPrimaryInverse is false. This makes such return value be in USD’s decimals that is 8.
